title: rangeBetween (Window) - Azure Databricks
display_title: Define rangeBetween frames for Window in PySpark
description: Documentation for the Window\.rangeBetween method in PySpark.
summary: Creates aWindowSpecwith the frame boundaries defined, fromstart(inclusive) toend(inclusive). Bothstartandendare relative from the current row. For example,0means "current row",-1means one before the current row, and5means five after the current row. A range-based boundary is based on the actual value of the ORDER BY expression(s). An offset alters the value of the ORDER BY expression — for example, if the current ORDER BY value is10and the lower bound offset is-3, the resulting lower bound is7.
classification_reason: Explains detailed semantics of rangeBetween, including relative offsets and how ORDER BY values are adjusted, which is precise API behavior.

This per-workspace URL has the format adb-<workspace-id>.<random-number>.azuredatabricks.net The per-workspace URL replaces the deprecated regional URL (<region>.azuredatabricks.net) to access workspaces. Important Avoid using legacy regional URLs. They may not work for new workspaces, are less reliable, and exhibit lower performance than per-workspace URLs.
classification_reason: Describes per-workspace URL format and deprecation of regional URLs; contains a URL pattern but not limits, configs, or decision matrices.

title: Workspace browser - Azure Databricks
description: Learn how to create, view, and organize objects in the workspace browser.
summary: With the workspace browser you can create, browse, and organize Azure Databricks objects, including notebooks, libraries, experiments, queries, dashboards, and alerts, in a single place. You can then share objects and assign permissions at the folder level to organize objects by team or project. You can also browse content in Databricks Git folders. The workspace browser introduces a contextual browser that allows you to browse content, including content in Git folders, from within a notebook.
classification_reason: Describes workspace browser usage and organization; likely procedural without config tables or expert-only details.
